```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

dir="${1:?usage: $0 directory days [--run]}"
days="${2:-7}"
mode="${3:---dry-run}"

find "$dir" -xdev -type f -name '*.log' -mtime +"$days" -print0 |
while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
 if [[ "$mode" == "--run" ]]; then
 gzip -n "$file"

done
```

Write a Bash script that reports local users with expired accounts.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

today="$(date +%s)"

awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 && $3 < 65534 {print $1}' /etc/passwd |
while read -r user; do
 expiry="$(sudo chage -l "$user" | awk -F: '/Account expires/ {gsub(/^ /,"",$2); print $2}')"
 [[ "$expiry" == "never" ]] && continue
 if exp_ts="$(date -d "$expiry" +%s 2>/dev/null)"; then
 if (( exp_ts < today )); then
 echo "EXPIRED $user $expiry"
 fi
 fi
```

```bash
 'addresses': run(['ip','-br','addr','show',iface]),

}, separators=(',',':')))
PY
```

Back up system and user crontabs on a Debian server.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
out="/backup/cron-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
sudo install -d -m 0700 "$out"
sudo cp -a /etc/crontab /etc/cron.d /etc/cron.daily /etc/cron.hourly /etc/cron.monthly /etc/cron.weekly "$out" 2>/dev/null || true
compgen -u | while read -r user; do sudo crontab -l -u "$user" > "$out/user-$user.cron" 2>/dev/null || true; done
```
This captures system cron locations and readable user crontabs into a timestamped directory.
